statement: Unconditional request patching and unconditional target-snapshot refresh are a major write-amplifier, even when business state is unchanged.
rationale: patchRequest always writes and refreshes snapshots; duplicate apply branch always patches success again; telemetry shows large duplicate event volume with same request entities.
keyAssumptions: No-op field equality checks are currently absent in request and snapshot updates.
Repeated apply invocations include cases where request status already equals success.
validationCriteria: After no-op guards, llm_requests patch count per completed window decreases materially (>50%).
process_request_targets update count per completed window decreases materially (>50%).
critiquePoints: No-op guard alone may not fix duplicate provider calls from concurrent workers.
Telemetry volume can remain high if duplicate events are still emitted intentionally.
questions: Do we want to preserve duplicate-success events for forensic visibility or collapse them?
Which fields should trigger snapshot refresh vs be ignored?
